Leader.name: Commander Hawk
Leader.title: Field Commander
Leader.tagline: Hit hard. Hit fast. Don't stop.
Leader.lore: Named after his father, James Hawk joined the Air Force after his father passed away and learned to fly the Pterodactylus Gunship. Over years of service he became known both for his mastery of the gunship and for his bravery against unfavorable odds, including flying through a hurricane to save a civilian transport ship from the CoRP. For his bravery and selflessness, President Enhet added him to the cabinet as head of the Department of Defense (DOD). Hawk's lifelong love of Earth's ancient flying reptiles led him to design the Quetzalcoatlus Gunship, built on years of flying and upgrading his own Pterodactylus Gunship.

Leader.name: President Enhet
Leader.title: Political Leader
Leader.lore: A once high-ranking military general renowned for ending the Final War and bringing a lasting era of peace. The final battle was an all-or-nothing assault born of the CoRP's desperation amid diminishing supplies and public war exhaustion. Enhet's tactics and militaristic genius marshaled a spearhead of 10 million united soldiers that pierced the GTD (Great Tyrannus Dominion) capital and ended the war. His front-line bravery earned him the highest honor of leadership when the UDF was formed, and he became its President. His soldier's background drove revolutionary guerrilla tactics for safer, faster movement and ambush, and inspired the Groundbreaker (a mobile factory that tunnels underground to reach strategic locations) and the Groundhog (a stealth unit that waits underground to ambush passing forces and gather intel).

Leader.name: IQ (Iris Quantum)
Leader.title: Tech / Intel Lead
Leader.tagline: Information is the only weapon that never runs out of ammo.
Leader.lore: Born into a family of researchers who studied genetic engineering, Iris grew up immersed in the field. She developed strong genetic-engineering skills but grew increasingly fascinated by the manipulation of space itself, inspired by the ancient alien ruins scattered across the galaxy. Her work caught President Enhet's attention, who saw military applications in it. This led to quantum-biological augmentation, which grants abilities to buff troopers and vehicles and project barriers and shielding, but at a cost: the energy exerted can cause intense hunger or, if overused, death. If an augmented individual dies, the unstable quantum energy within their body detonates in an explosive release. Despite the risks, Enhet authorized the Quantum Augmentation Program, allowing specially augmented humans to serve in the military alongside temporary augmentations.
